  "findings": [
    "Primary and secondary windings are landed in reverse, creating an extremely dangerous step-up hazard instead of the intended step-down configuration."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Lock out and tag out the transformer immediately.",
    "Disconnect the improperly landed primary and secondary conductors.",
    "Re-terminate the conductors to their correct respective terminals according to the manufacturer's schematic and submittals."
  ],
  "rationale": "Landing the primary and secondary connections in reverse on a step-down transformer will cause it to act as a step-up transformer when energized, resulting in catastrophic overvoltage, equipment destruction, and severe arc flash or shock hazards.",
  "references": [
    "NEC Art. 450",
    "NEC 250.30"
  ]
